trans.explain: The user said “barcode” · the ASR wrote “vargoor”. A field-support ticket keyed on a wrong barcode fails silently. This is exactly the kind of miss only a human ear catches.

trans.context: क्या अभी बात कर सकती हैं?
trans.explain: The user said “नहीं” · the ASR heard the English word “ring”. Code-switched speech trips ASR constantly; the correction keeps meaning intact.

trans.context: क्या मेरी बात हो रही है?
trans.explain: The ASR got this one right. Saying “Correct” when it is correct matters as much as catching errors · false alarms poison the dataset.

pron.explain: The agent mispronounced the city “Surat”. City names are one of the top reasons clients buy the pronunciation-audit dataset · the model has to say them the way locals do.
The agent mispronounced the caller's name “Ananya”. Getting a customer's own name wrong on the first line breaks trust instantly · this is a proper-noun miss.
